title: Community Resilience After the July Earthquakes
body: In July 2025, San Cristóbal El Alto and neighboring communities throughout Sacatepéquez experienced a powerful series of earthquakes that were felt across the Antigua Valley. While the tremors caused concern and minor structural damage in parts of the region, residents once again demonstrated the resilience and unity that have defined these highland communities for generations. Today, life continues with renewed appreciation for the strength of the people and the beauty of the landscape they call home.

title: Earthquakes Remind Us of Guatemala's Living Landscape
body: The July 2025 earthquake sequence served as a powerful reminder that the Antigua Valley sits within one of the world's most dynamic volcanic and seismic regions. Surrounded by Volcán de Agua, Acatenango, and Fuego, communities like San Cristóbal El Alto have adapted to the forces of nature for centuries. Despite the challenges posed by the tremors and aftershocks, the village continues to welcome visitors while honoring a history shaped by both resilience and renewal.
